course_title: Biological Foundations of Behavior: Appld Anim Beh & Welf in Zoos
subject: PSYC
course_description: Selected issues in physiological and comparative psychology.
course_notes: Prior completion of PSYC 110 (or equivalent). Juniors and seniors only; others, by permission of the instructor only. The emphasis of this course is direct exposure and experience with current issues and developments in research and animal management in zoos. We will use multiple examples to study a range of topics, from applied animal behavior to animal welfare, and from population management to conservation. Using a variety of literature sources, we will discuss these topics as a group. To engage in direct experience, we will meet at Zoo Atlanta to explore multiple case studies. We will be working with animal curators and animal caretakers to gain insights into various aspects of the personnel infrastructure of zoos. Even if you are not considering zoos as an institution for possible employment, this course will provide valuable experiences that will have utility in other career areas, such as teaching, field work, and research. Ultimately, employment in most AZA-accredited zoos requires both a university degree and zoo-related experience. A course on zoos is an appropriate and important step to obtain university credit and zoo experience. All classes will be held on the grounds of Zoo Atlanta. Dress appropriately for daily weather, as many activities will be outdoors. Week 1 we will meet outside the front gate of Zoo Atlanta at 9am sharp. On subsequent days, your name will be on the guest list at the front gate. Show your ID to the attendant and then proceed to our designated meeting room.
